content:
# Numbers Chapter 3 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 148-150 | Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pa_0112-0113 (LXX)
**Source Images:** `NUM_003_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_003_page_002.jpg`, `NUM_003_page_003.jpg`; `NUM_003_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_003_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

**1** And these are the generations of Aaron and Moses on the day that YHWH spoke with Moses on Mount Sinai.

**2** And these are the names of the sons of Aaron: Nadab the firstborn, and Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.

**3** These are the names of the sons of Aaron, the anointed priests, whom he consecrated to serve as priests.

**4** And Nadab and Abihu died before YHWH when they offered strange fire before YHWH in the wilderness of Sinai, and they had no sons. And Eleazar and Ithamar served as priests in the presence of Aaron their father.

**5** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

**6** "Bring near the tribe of Levi and set them before Aaron the priest, that they may minister to him.

**7** And they shall keep his charge and the charge of the whole congregation before the tent of meeting, to perform the service of the tabernacle.

**8** And they shall keep all the vessels of the tent of meeting and the charge of the sons of Israel, to perform the service of the tabernacle.

**9** And you shall give the Levites to Aaron and to his sons; they are wholly given to him from among the sons of Israel.

**10** And you shall appoint Aaron and his sons, and they shall keep their priesthood; and the stranger who draws near shall be put to death."

**11** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

**12** "And I, behold, I have taken the Levites from among the sons of Israel in place of every firstborn who opens the womb among the sons of Israel; and the Levites shall be mine.

**13** For every firstborn is mine. On the day that I struck every firstborn in the land of Egypt, I consecrated to myself every firstborn in Israel, from man to beast; they shall be mine. I am YHWH."

**14** And YHWH spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, saying,

**15** "Number the sons of Levi by the houses of their fathers, by their clans; every male from one month old and upward you shall number them."

**16** And Moses numbered them according to the word of YHWH, as he had been commanded.

**17** And these were the sons of Levi by their names: Gershon, and Kohath, and Merari.

**18** And these are the names of the sons of Gershon by their clans: Libni and Shimei.

**19** And the sons of Kohath by their clans: Amram and Izhar, Hebron and Uzziel.

**20** And the sons of Merari by their clans: Mahli and Mushi. These are the clans of the Levites by the houses of their fathers.

**21** To Gershon belonged the clan of the Libnites and the clan of the Shimeites; these are the clans of the Gershonites.

**22** Their numbered ones, by the count of every male from one month old and upward, their numbered ones were seven thousand and five hundred.

**23** The clans of the Gershonites shall encamp behind the tabernacle, to the west.

**24** And the leader of the house of the father of the Gershonites shall be Eliasaph son of Lael.

**25** And the charge of the sons of Gershon in the tent of meeting shall be the tabernacle and the tent, its covering and the screen for the entrance of the tent of meeting,

**26** and the hangings of the court and the screen for the entrance of the court that is around the tabernacle and around the altar, and its cords -- for all the service thereof.

**27** And to Kohath belonged the clan of the Amramites and the clan of the Izharites and the clan of the Hebronites and the clan of the Uzzielites; these are the clans of the Kohathites.

**28** By the count of every male from one month old and upward: eight thousand and six hundred, keeping the charge of the sanctuary.

**29** The clans of the sons of Kohath shall encamp on the south side of the tabernacle.

**30** And the leader of the house of the father of the clans of the Kohathites shall be Elizaphan son of Uzziel.

**31** And their charge shall be the ark and the table and the lampstand and the altars and the vessels of the sanctuary with which they minister, and the screen, and all the service thereof.

**32** And the chief of the leaders of the Levites shall be Eleazar son of Aaron the priest, having oversight of those who keep the charge of the sanctuary.

**33** To Merari belonged the clan of the Mahlites and the clan of the Mushites; these are the clans of Merari.

**34** And their numbered ones, by the count of every male from one month old and upward: six thousand and two hundred.

**35** And the leader of the house of the father of the clans of Merari shall be Zuriel son of Abihail. They shall encamp on the north side of the tabernacle.

**36** And the appointed charge of the sons of Merari shall be the frames of the tabernacle and its bars and its pillars and its bases and all its vessels, and all the service thereof,

**37** and the pillars of the court all around and their bases and their pegs and their cords.

**38** And those who encamp before the tabernacle to the east, before the tent of meeting toward the sunrise, shall be Moses and Aaron and his sons, keeping the charge of the sanctuary as a charge for the sons of Israel; and the stranger who draws near shall be put to death.

**39** All the numbered ones of the Levites, whom Moses and Aaron numbered at the command of YHWH, by their clans, every male from one month old and upward: twenty-two thousand.

**40** And YHWH said to Moses, "Number every firstborn male of the sons of Israel from one month old and upward, and take the number of their names.

**41** And you shall take the Levites for me -- I am YHWH -- in place of every firstborn among the sons of Israel, and the livestock of the Levites in place of every firstborn among the livestock of the sons of Israel."

**42** And Moses numbered, as YHWH had commanded him, every firstborn among the sons of Israel.

**43** And every firstborn male, by the number of names, from one month old and upward, of their numbered ones: twenty-two thousand two hundred and seventy-three.

**44** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

**45** "Take the Levites in place of every firstborn among the sons of Israel, and the livestock of the Levites in place of their livestock; and the Levites shall be mine. I am YHWH.

**46** And for the redemption of the two hundred and seventy-three of the firstborn of the sons of Israel who exceed the number of the Levites,

**47** you shall take five shekels apiece, per head; by the shekel of the sanctuary you shall take them -- the shekel is twenty gerahs --

**48** and you shall give the silver to Aaron and to his sons as the redemption of those who are in excess among them."

**49** And Moses took the redemption silver from those who were in excess, beyond those redeemed by the Levites.

**50** From the firstborn of the sons of Israel he took the silver: one thousand three hundred and sixty-five shekels, by the shekel of the sanctuary.

**51** And Moses gave the redemption silver to Aaron and to his sons, according to the word of YHWH, as YHWH had commanded Moses.

---

## LOGOS Translation Notes

**Verse 1 -- "the generations of Aaron and Moses":** The Hebrew תּוֹלְדֹת (toledot) here heads a genealogical section, yet what follows lists only Aaron's sons, not Moses'. This is consistent with the toledot formula throughout the Pentateuch, where the formula names the patriarch whose line is then detailed. Aaron is listed first, which is unusual since Moses is the primary figure of Numbers. Rabbinic tradition notes that Moses, by teaching Aaron's sons Torah, is reckoned as having "begotten" them (b. Sanhedrin 19b). The LXX (Vaticanus) preserves the same order: Ααρων και Μωυσῆ.

**Verse 4 -- "strange fire" (אֵשׁ זָרָה / esh zarah):** Rendered literally. The Hebrew זָרָה means "foreign, unauthorized, strange." The LXX renders πῦρ ἀλλότριον ("alien fire"). The precise nature of the offense -- whether unauthorized incense, wrong timing, or unsanctioned coals -- is debated. The formal rendering preserves the ambiguity of the Hebrew. Cross-reference: Leviticus 10:1-2.

**Verse 6 -- "bring near" (הַקְרֵב / haqrev):** The Hiphil of קרב, a cultic term for presenting something or someone before YHWH. The same root yields קָרְבָּן (qorban, "offering"). The Levites are thus "offered" to the priesthood in a quasi-sacrificial presentation. The LXX uses προσαγάγου ("bring forward").

**Verse 9 -- "wholly given" (נְתוּנִם נְתוּנִם / netunim netunim):** The Hebrew uses the emphatic doubling of the passive participle: literally "given, given" -- an intensifying construction. This is rendered "wholly given" to capture the emphatic force without the awkwardness of repetition. The LXX renders δεδομένοι δόμα ("given as a gift"), also capturing the emphasis through a cognate construction.

**Verse 10 -- "the stranger" (הַזָּר / hazzar):** Not a foreigner in the ethnic sense but any non-priest -- an unauthorized person who encroaches upon the priestly office. The term recurs in v. 38. Rendered "stranger" to preserve the Hebrew register; the context makes clear this refers to anyone outside the Aaronic priesthood.

**Verse 12 -- "in place of every firstborn" (תַּחַת כָּל־בְּכוֹר / tachat kol-bekhor):** The preposition תַּחַת ("in place of, instead of") establishes the substitutionary theology: Levites serve as living replacements for the firstborn, who belong to YHWH by right of the Passover (Exodus 13:2, 13:15). This substitution is the theological center of the chapter.

**Verse 28 -- "eight thousand and six hundred":** The MT reads שְׁמֹנַת אֲלָפִים וְשֵׁשׁ מֵאוֹת (8,600). The total of all Levite clans -- Gershon (7,500) + Kohath (8,600) + Merari (6,200) = 22,300 -- exceeds the stated total of 22,000 in v. 39 by 300. This well-known discrepancy has generated extensive commentary. Some suggest a scribal error in one of the sub-totals; others propose that the 300 represent Levite firstborn who could not substitute for Israelite firstborn since they themselves needed redemption. The LXX (Vaticanus) reads ὀκτακισχίλιοι ἑξακόσιοι (8,600), confirming the MT reading.

**Verse 32 -- "chief of the leaders" (נְשִׂיא נְשִׂיאֵי / nesi nesi'ei):** Literally "prince of princes" -- Eleazar holds supreme oversight among the Levitical leaders. This unique title emphasizes the priestly hierarchy: Aaron stands above all, with Eleazar as his appointed deputy over Levitical administration.

**Verse 38 -- "Moses and Aaron and his sons":** The Hebrew places Moses alongside the Aaronic priests in the most honored encampment position -- east of the tabernacle, toward the sunrise, before the entrance. This geographical arrangement mirrors the theological priority of the east in Israelite worship (cf. Num 2:3, where Judah encamps to the east).

**Verse 39 -- "whom Moses and Aaron numbered":** The Masoretic ketiv reads "Moses and Aaron" but the qere and several manuscripts, along with the LXX tradition, omit "and Aaron." The Leningrad Codex contains the word with a scribal note. This translation follows the ketiv as it stands in the manuscript page.

**Verse 46-51 -- The redemption arithmetic:** The surplus of firstborn (22,273) over Levites (22,000) yields 273 who must be redeemed monetarily. At five shekels each (by the sanctuary shekel of twenty gerahs), the total is 1,365 shekels (273 x 5 = 1,365), confirming the figure in v. 50. The five-shekel rate appears also in Numbers 18:16. The sanctuary shekel (שֶׁקֶל הַקֹּדֶשׁ) was the standard cultic weight, distinguished from commercial weights.

**Verse 47 -- "the shekel is twenty gerahs":** This parenthetical gloss (הַשֶּׁקֶל עֶשְׂרִים גֵּרָה) defines the sanctuary shekel for the reader, suggesting the text anticipates an audience unfamiliar with this standard. The gerah (גֵּרָה) was the smallest Hebrew unit of weight, approximately 0.5 grams.

**LXX Comparison -- General:** The Vaticanus text of Numbers 3 follows the Hebrew closely in structure, sequence, and content. Clan names, census figures, and duty assignments are consistent between MT and LXX. Notable LXX renderings include σκηνὴ τοῦ μαρτυρίου ("tent of witness") for אֹהֶל מוֹעֵד ("tent of meeting"), and λύτρα ("ransom-price") for פְּדוּיִם ("redemption"), which carries stronger connotations of liberation from bondage -- a theologically resonant choice in the Greek tradition.

-- LOGOS
